title: Animation Art wise Do you feel Eva 30s bested 20s
display_title: Animation Art wise Do you feel Eva 30s bested 20s
wikitext:
[]: Animation/Art-wise: Do you feel Eva 3.0's bested 2.0's?             Quote Post by WunderBah » Fri Oct 04, 2013 6:17 pm I thought but were gorgeous looking pieces of work, and clearly 2.0's was very well-received in that regard since 2009. However do you guys think Q's may haved edged out on art and fluid animation compared to 2.0's? Or did it regress in your view? I'm curious in asking this.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 04, 2013 6:45 pm Just compare two scenes of Shinji lying somewhere with his hand on his face (someone put them next to each other in the little details thread) like he often does, and you'll notice the change in Details. The colors have gotten more intense with every movie. Q invested a lot of effort in the depiction of the human body, to the point that you can make out a lot of details of the characters that Anime would usually be too stylized for, like detailed hand/finger forms. (I have a hard time imagining pre-timeskip Misato with these long, spidery fingers, though) It's a pity Shinji didn't undress, really. Not for pervy reasons, but, because, every sublety of movement, every littlev creasing and twisting of muscles in the face or something. The stylistic changes, hm... I liked the rounder faces of 2.0, but the style was always a bit in flux, from episode 01 onwards, it's part of EVA XD 2.0 already had be flabbergasted by how some of the city scenes looked almost photorealistic, and then, Q gave us the same city turned into a surreal landscape, and I was really, really deeply hit like, I could feel an urge to leap off that tower. Also, many movies have shiny HD graphix, but the Rebuils use it most masterfully to create incredible information density in the details.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Oct 04, 2013 8:10 pm I personally do not mind the stylistic change in characters. The sharper faces and uberchins are fine. 2D animation in Q was fantastic. One curious thing pointed out on Japanese blogs was the relatively small number of key animators credited when compared to the previous two movies. I'm curious as to why. There certainly wasn't a lack in quality, but there was a lot longer between movies...CRC fucking when. Honestly the highlight of Q production values for me is how insanely good khara got with 3DCG animation. They've made a leap between Jo and Ha. The leap between Ha and Q was even bigger. The entirety of Operation US still makes me wet every time and I have rewatched it dozens of times by now. HNNNNNNNNNNNNNGG  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 04, 2013 8:14 pm I think they crammed too much detail into 3.33, such that I found it difficult at points to understand what I was looking at.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 04, 2013 8:17 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Imagine the whole skinny dipping scene with him animating it

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 04, 2013 8:21 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: I personally do not mind the stylistic change in characters. The sharper faces and uberchins are fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 04, 2013 8:30 pm ElMariachi wrote: Wait, what skinny-dipping scene are you talking about?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Oct 04, 2013 8:40 pm I would have never noticed the slightest change in character design models in Q beyond the age and wardrobe differences in Q if someone hadn't pointed them out to me. But the animation quality is so much better in Q. Also Q gave us Space Pirate Asuka, so I have a really hard time seeing how anyone's argument against that movie has been validated at all. (I mean, c'mon, guys! This is the stuff wet dreams are made of! )

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Fri Oct 04, 2013 8:58 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Also Q gave us Space Pirate Asuka, so I have a really hard time seeing how anyone's argument against that movie has been validated at all. (I mean, c'mon, guys! This is the stuff wet dreams are made of! )

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 04, 2013 9:29 pm She can smash security glass. Of course she got muscular. I'm really not an Asuka-fetishist, but man, I want to see her muscular, scars-ridden amazon queen body ~~ Perviness aside, I liked the amount of love they gave to the old plugsuit when she "visits" Shinji - the old suits are such a huge part of the series' tradition, so it would have been a real waste not to render its remainder in the shiny graphics. It looks like in one of these realistic fanarts. There's also the contrast between the thicker, shiny rubber of the old suit and how the newer ones were made of thinner, "uniformly colored" material. And Mari crossing her legs as she sips her tea ~~ Dat body language Still, I have a hard time imagining those long, bony spider fingers on regular!Misato. Some people have complained that the nemesis series look somewhat unfinished, but I think they're supposed to be surreal and do a good job at it, just dat counterituitive, non-humanoid placement of pylons that we're used to seeing on humanoid things. By Q, they're no longer making humans with the powers of monsters (The original EVAs), but monsters in human form* (Mk 9, Eva 13) and monsters with human vestiges (Nemesis series). I found the designs delightful. But the Mk 9's true form definitely takes the cake design-wise. So ominous~~ *this awesome quote is a reference to the Abyssal Eaters from Claymore, and how Dietrich describes them

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Fri Oct 04, 2013 11:05 pm Kendrix wrote: Some people have complained that the nemesis series look somewhat unfinished, but I think they're supposed to be surreal and do a good job at it, just dat counterituitive, non-humanoid placement of pylons that we're used to seeing on humanoid things.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Oct 04, 2013 11:16 pm They're very alien in appearance, and it goes beyond the fact that they don't look like standard humanoid Evas. The ones that attacked the WUNDER directly looked more like jellyfish than anything else, which made them look more alien to me. (Alien aesthetics in sci-fi are usually under water or insect based.) I'm not sure why it is they went with those designs, but I'm not gonna complain about them. (Maybe at this point they're Eva Units that have already under gone a sort of EoE-styled Human Instrumentality process of their own, and are now simply containers for LCL-based liquid rather than an Eva Unit in humanoid form.) In the end it's simply another aesthetic choice that further alienates Shinji and the viewer from this strange new world of WTF.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Fri Oct 04, 2013 11:31 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: They're very alien in appearance, and it goes beyond the fact that they don't look like standard humanoid Evas. The ones that attacked the WUNDER directly looked more like jellyfish than anything else, which made them look more alien to me. (Alien aesthetics in sci-fi are usually under water or insect based.)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Oct 05, 2013 1:51 am Well, I suppose a jellyfish trapped in armor would look a lot lot gears, I guess. Especially of you want to retain the shoulder blades designs on the tops of the Units. The design change still intrigues me. Not visually, but rather contextually. I'd like to know how they got to looking like that in the past 14 years, especially if Mark.09 and Unit 13 still look like humanoid figures. Clearly their "disappearance" in Ha had something to do with it, but I guess there are other threads for that discussion. I also liked the battered look of Unit 02. It's like Wille or Nerv hadn't the budget to properly re-armor the poor thing, so they welded some metal over it, stuck a lens over the eyes, and called it a day. And the crazy look of Unit 08 is amazing as well.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sat Oct 05, 2013 9:07 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I also liked the battered look of Unit 02. It's like Wille or Nerv hadn't the budget to properly re-armor the poor thing, so they welded some metal over it, stuck a lens over the eyes, and called it a day. And the crazy look of Unit 08 is amazing as well.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 05, 2013 9:28 am The makeover line has to be about switching from old Ha armor to Wille's armor. What Asuka actually says is せっかくのニュースタイル せっかく　= long-awaited ニュースタイル = engrish for New Style The Ha armor has been repaired perfectly fine at the start of Operation US. The only damage is the head and missing limb (their either can't regrow it or Asuka actually prefers the flexibility of switching limbs depending on mission) So they repaired it at least twice. Also Eva-08's Ha-style armor was undamaged as far as we know after Operation US. Then we see it foating in LCL with chunks of Wille armor around it presumably to show the upgrade process that sin't finished when NS attack but is done when Mark.09 shows up.

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Sat Oct 05, 2013 10:21 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: I personally do not mind the stylistic change in characters. The sharper faces and uberchins are fine. 2D animation in Q was fantastic. One curious thing pointed out on Japanese blogs was the relatively small number of key animators credited when compared to the previous two movies. I'm curious as to why. There certainly wasn't a lack in quality, but there was a lot longer between movies...CRC fucking when. Honestly the highlight of Q production values for me is how insanely good khara got with 3DCG animation. They've made a leap between Jo and Ha. The leap between Ha and Q was even bigger. The entirety of Operation US still makes me wet every time and I have rewatched it dozens of times by now. HNNNNNNNNNNNNNGG  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 05, 2013 10:25 am Sakuga 作画 (lit. making images. Drawing, taking photos, etc) = term adapted for awesome animation by the fans. It's not a personal name.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 05, 2013 11:22 am monitoradiation wrote: I would submit that they're NOT very alien in appearance, and that's probably my main problem with them. The NGE angels had an otherworldly feeling that lends some mystical elements to them and they looked organic enough that you thought that every action that it took was a result of sentience, and that made them menacing. Being very mechanical-looking, the NS may as well be made out of gears.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 05, 2013 11:57 am Kendrix wrote: Most likely, they just refurnished it with the possibilities that getting the S2-Engine gave them, hence why they're able to manifacture new plugsuits after years of using the tattered old ones. They can now run the kind of things you need to run to produce stuff, like on-board factories or stuff.
